header.title: Select the header
header.tooltip: Draw around the bank's logo / name at the top of the page. This zone acts as a fingerprint: next time you import a statement from the same bank, Athena will recognize the template automatically.

table.title: Select the transactions table
table.tooltip: Draw around the entire transactions table, including the column header. Don't include totals or the page footer — just the movement rows.

date.title: Select the Date column
date.tooltip: Draw a thin vertical band that covers only the date column, inside the table you just outlined. The height doesn't matter — Athena only uses the left/right bounds.

description.title: Select the Description column
description.tooltip: Draw the description column (merchant name / memo). It can be wide — neighboring columns are ignored thanks to each column's own bounds.

amount.title: Select the Amount column
amount.tooltip: First choose whether your bank shows a single signed amount (+ / −) or two Debit + Credit columns. Then draw the matching columns. Finally, name the template so you can find it again later.

headerStep.prompt: Step 1/{{total}} — Select the header <1>(used to recognize this bank next time)</1>.

tableStep.prompt: Step 2/{{total}} — Select the transactions table <1>(every row, including the column header)</1>.
tableStep.repeatsLabel: The table repeats on every page
tableStep.pagesHeading: Pages to import for this account
tableStep.pagesHint: If the statement contains several accounts, only tick the pages that belong to the account chosen at upload. Other pages are ignored for this import.

dateStep.prompt: Step 3/{{total}} — Draw the <1>Date</1> column <2>inside the table — the next step starts automatically</2>.

descriptionStep.prompt: Step 4/{{total}} — Draw the <1>Description</1> column <2>(the transaction's description — the next step starts automatically)</2>.

anchorPicker.heading: Identify your account
anchorPicker.emptyHint: No header candidate was detected on this PDF. Automatic detection will still run — you'll be able to check it in the <1>PDF Templates</1> panel after the import.
anchorPicker.hint: Tick "Mine" on your account's header. Every other detected header will automatically be treated as a different account (uncheck one if that's not the case). With no selection, the server tries to guess from the pages ticked above.

extractedTextPanel.hint: What pdfjs extracted from the PDF, page by page. Highlighted lines look like an account header (server-side heuristic) and become candidates for the account / other-accounts marker.

ocrProgress.title: Recognizing characters
ocrProgress.loadError: Couldn't load the recognized text — try again.
ocrProgress.retryHint: Close this window and try again with a sharper or better-framed file.

preview.heading: Preview
preview.noRows: No rows extracted. Check that the columns cover the table correctly.

staleBanner.title: The previous template no longer matches this PDF
staleBanner.fallbackDiagnostic: The template was applied but produced no rows. Reconfigure the zones below.
staleBanner.replaceNote: The existing template will be replaced by this new version when you click "{{importLabel}}".
